pattern: Dynamic programming over remaining moves and board position accumulates the probability of staying on the board, since each of the eight knight jumps is equally likely.
hints: Track the probability of being on each cell after a given number of moves.
Each move spreads a cell's probability equally to its eight knight destinations, and jumps off the board are lost.
Iterate move by move, updating a probability grid; the answer is the total probability summed over all on-board cells after k moves.

edge_inputs.description: statement example two no moves on a unit board
tiny board where every move leaves it

stress.description: random board size, move count, and start cell within bounds
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 12)
    k = rng.randint(0, min(size, 40))
    row = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
    column = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
    return [n, k, row, column]
